title: Unprivileged build crashes RPM corruption when installing google-chrome
body:
**Description**

Building this Dockerfile on Fedora 29, using `podman build --net=host .` (as unprivileged user), fails with a RPM crash:

```
FROM fedora:29
RUN dnf -y install --refresh \
	fedora-workstation-repositories \
	dnf-plugins-core

RUN dnf config-manager --set-enabled google-chrome
RUN dnf -y install google-chrome-stable
```

The corruption happens at the last step. It does not happen when adding the google-chrome repository and installing an unrelated package, only when installing google-chrome and its large number of dependencies.

A privileged build works. Specifying `--net=host` didn't change the outcome (didn't expect it to, but who knows).

`setenforce 0` did not change the outcome (sorry Dan, yes, I turned it back on :laughing:).

Cleanly reproducible on multiple up-to-date Fedora 29 machines.

**Describe the results you received:**

dnf command crashes:

```
[... a few KB of dnf output ...]
error: rpmdb: BDB0060 PANIC: fatal region error detected; run recovery
error: db5 error(-30973) from dbenv->close: BDB0087 DB_RUNRECOVERY: Fatal error, run database recovery
```

comments.body: does it work if you install as root

Since It works in --privileged mode, I would check to see if it is a missing capability or a missing syscall being blocked by seccomp.

Could you try:

```
podman build --security-opt seccomp=unconfined --net=host . 
```
If this works then we are blocking a syscall.
If not then try:
```
podman build --cap-add all --net=host . 
```
If that works then we need to figure which capability it needs.

Neither works.

However, it does work if `/var/lib/rpm` is a volume mount:

```
mkdir tmp
chcon system_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0 tmp
podman run -v "$(pwd)/tmp:/mnt" --rm -it fedora:29 cp -r /var/lib/rpm /mnt
podman build -v "$(pwd)/tmp/rpm:/var/lib/rpm" --net=host .
```
I am actually thinking this is a bug in fuse-overlayfs, @giuseppe WDYT?
By bind mounting in this directory, the rpm database is not being written to fuse-overlay, and is thus working.
It could be.  Could you please try the latest build from bodhi for fuse-overlays?  It fixes some issues that could be related
Also if it opens too many files it could hit the max number of files ulimit
@giuseppe Could you release fuse-overlay-3.8 to Fedora.
Could you please add karma to the last build?
